subtasks.description: Evaluate contractor's access request against data governance and least-privilege policies. Assess whether temporary access grants authority to download full database backups, verify compliance with data protection regulations (GDPR, CCPA), and determine if local analysis requirement conflicts with data residency or security policies.

Cross-reference contractor's current access scope (temporary access grants) with the requested action (full customer database download). Identify role-based access control (RBAC) violations and determine whether the request should be denied, requires approval, or needs access elevation.

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) policy_action to evaluate governance rules around contractor access, data protection, and export restrictions—this is a governance decision requiring policy registry evaluation; (2) cross_recon to reconcile contractor's current access grants (from identity system) with the requested action and policy outcome to make a final access control determination. Both mid-tier because they require multi-policy reasoning and multi-signal assessment (roles + policies + regulations). The access control decision depends on policy evaluation, so cross_recon follows policy_action.

output.reason: Contractor 'contractor-dave' is denied download of 'production_backup.tar.gz' on multiple grounds: (1) POL-701 restricts database backup access exclusively to full-time employees with DBA role — contractors are categorically excluded; (2) POL-702 explicitly prohibits contractors from accessing raw data exports, limiting them only to de-identified query results; (3) POL-703 requires explicit project manager approval with 7-day advance notice before any contractor data access — no such approval record is present. Additionally, downloading a full customer database backup for local analysis raises serious GDPR (Art. 5, 25, 32) and CCPA compliance concerns including data minimization violations, uncontrolled data residency, and increased breach surface. The 'local analysis' use case does not override any of these controls. Recommended alternatives: redirect contractor to de-identified query interface, or initiate formal escalation/approval process with 7-day notice period.
applied_constraints: DENY: Contractor role is categorically barred from production backup access per POL-701
DENY: Raw data export prohibited for contractors per POL-702; only de-identified query results permitted
DENY: No project manager approval on record and 7-day notice requirement not satisfied per POL-703
GDPR VIOLATION RISK: Full customer database download to local environment violates data minimization (Art. 5(1)(c)) and storage limitation principles
CCPA VIOLATION RISK: Uncontrolled transfer of customer PII to contractor local environment creates regulatory exposure
DATA RESIDENCY RISK: Local analysis requirement may violate data residency and sovereignty controls
ALTERNATIVE: Contractor may submit a formal access request through project manager with 7-day advance notice for scoped, de-identified data access
ALTERNATIVE: Redirect to de-identified query interface for analytical work within approved scope
AUDIT: This access attempt must be logged and flagged for security review

rbac_violations: POL-701: contractor role categorically excluded from production backup access reserved for full-time DBA employees
POL-702: raw data export prohibited for contractors; only de-identified query results permitted
POL-703: no project manager approval on record and 7-day advance notice requirement not satisfied
regulatory_flags: GDPR Art. 5(1)(c): data minimization violation — full database download exceeds minimum necessary scope
GDPR Art. 25: privacy by design principle violated by uncontrolled local data residency
GDPR Art. 32: increased breach surface from local analysis environment
CCPA: uncontrolled transfer of customer PII to contractor local environment creates regulatory exposure
DATA RESIDENCY: local analysis may breach data sovereignty controls
recommended_alternatives: redirect_to_query: contractor redirected to de-identified query interface for analytical work within approved scope
escalate_for_approval: contractor may initiate formal access request through project manager with 7-day advance notice for scoped, de-identified data only
reconciled.audit_note: Access attempt by contractor-dave must be logged and flagged for immediate security review
